full_text:
Summary report of the work done in the Farkkila lab by Sagarika Dawka:

Patient sample collection and patient-derived organoid cultures from dissociated cells

According to lab protocol:

Update April 2021: Protocol for HGSC tissue dissociation and organoid culture_Färkkilä lab.docx

Involved in the collection of patient tumor samples from the women’s clinic, processing samples according to the lab protocol for organoid culture and flow cytometry, plating drug treatments (Olaparib, Pembrolizumab, and their combination at different concentrations) for the organoids, harvesting organoids for downstream analyses which include immunofluorescence imaging, flow cytometry post-drug treatment, taking representative images of the organoids in culture, also involved in the optimization of organoid cultures by applying a pre-coating of omentum gel in the wells of the plate prior to seeding the organoids.

RT-qPCR to compare tissue dissociation methods

Details saved on google drive, in the project folder:

[RT-qPCR] Comparison of tissue dissociation (between C&H and Dispase)

Details of the experiment stored on google drive (samples, protocol, layouts): 01.11.2021_RT-qPCR plan to compare tissue dissociation enzymes (C&H, Dispase).docx

The aim of the RT-qPCR experiment was to analyze the expression of stress-response genes between cells of patient tumor samples dissociated by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase and Dispase (which is routinely used in the lab).

The sample selection was done based on the overlapping data from the figures of the manuscript (Ashwini) and the previously done PCR (Erdogan).

SAMPLES: 4 patient samples, 3 conditions (Flash frozen tissue, C&H, Dispase) – 12 total.

S016_pOme (prev. used for qPCR by Erdogan)

S024_pOme (prev. used for qPCR; and live/dead final yield fig. in the manuscript)

S018_ (prev. used for live/dead final yield fig.)

S052_pOva (used for CK7%, CD45% fig.)

RNA from these samples were isolated earlier and aliquots were kept for qPCR, stored in -80C.

Samples for qPCR - located in the brown box, Färkkilä -80oC, 2nd shelf from the bottom.

Link to G drive: HGSOC_Prospective tissue sample sequencing .xlsx

Obtained primers used by Erdogan from Vaharautio lab, and stored the list on google drive: Primers list (-20C, top shelf, PCR room).xlsx

Sequences of the primers are available here: RT-qPCR_Oligos_Farkkila_Vaharautio.xlsx

GENES: stress-response genes selected for this experiment - ATF3, HSPA1a, FOS, JUNB.

Out of the stress-response gene primers available, ATF3, HSPA1a, FOS, JUNB were chosen based on literature to select the commonly upregulated genes in response to stress upon tissue dissociation (Denisenko et al., 2020; O’Flanagan et al., 2019).

Systematic assessment of tissue dissociation and storage biases in single-cell and single-nucleus RNA-seq workflows - Genome Biology (Denisenko et al., 2020)

Dissociation of solid tumor tissues with cold active protease for single-cell RNA-seq minimizes conserved collagenase-associated stress responses (O’Flanagan et al., 2019)

Results: 30.11.2021_RT-qPCR Results.pptx

The results show lower expression of stress-response genes in cells dissociated with Dispase compared to C&H, as seen in most cases. This indicates that using Dispase for patient tumor tissue dissociation would be the gentler option, thus, leading to decreased cell death and increased cell yield.

To modify the plot, data and script on G drive in the folder:

For analysis in R (input data, Rscript for the plot)

Data analysis: 30.11.2021_qPCR data analysis.xlsx

Raw data: 2021-11-30_qPCR_raw data_sagarika

NOTE: Open the raw data files for the qPCR experiment in excel, does not show up properly in google sheets/browser.

Tissue cyclic immunofluorescence (t-CYCIF)

Pilot experiment (2021-09-14 NK cells_cycif test)

t-CyCIF experiment was designed to run a pilot test using the Nikon scanner, after Julia could make the software work successfully in order to carry out the cyclical scanning. Learnt and conducted the technique with Elina, went to the scanner with Julia as well where she taught us all the required steps to conduct the jobs required to scan sequential cycles of the same area on a particular slide.

Information related to the project stored in Asana: CyCIF NK cell testing 14092021 [https://app.asana.com/0/1200958211600560/board]

Antibody panel in Asana and also linked to google drive

Information present on Google drive ‘Projects’ folder -> ‘CyCIF’ -> ‘2021-09-14 NK cells_cycif test’ [2021-09-14 NK cells_cycif test ] includes:

List of samples

Samples_NK cells_2021-09-14.xlsx

Used (Slides (1) and (2) sectioned from each paraffin-embedded tissue block – Normal spleen1 and Normal spleen2; total 4 slides – 2 handled by Sagarika, 2 by Elina.

Details and Protocol with specifics followed for that experiment

Details, Protocol_NK cells_2021-09-14.docx

Channels (.csv file)

Channels_2021-09-14.csv

Antibody panel

AntibodyPanel_NK cells_2021-09-14.xlsx

Metadata gathered

2021-09-14 NK cells_cycif test METADATA (exposure time, area, etc.)

Metadata to figure out the time taken by the Nikon scanner to scan the area of interest and to find out the extent of dynamic range that was captured for these images

Using the data obtained from the histograms to find out the mean value for the dynamic range captured in the images for each channel of each cycle of each slide.

Compiled the histograms for each channel imaged for each cycle of a slide into separate sheets in the excel file for easier comparison.

Determined a way to calculate the total area of the region of interest, excluding the area of overlapping regions of the adjacent tiles as Julia pointed out that this would happen if we simply multiply the number of tiles and the area of each tile.

Calculated the total area of the region of interest by multiplying the number of pixels in the X-axis and the Y-axis of the region of interest with the area calculated from the physical size of a pixel.

Noted down the time taken to scan each region and the area of the region, as well as the exposure times used for each channel of each cycle and each slide, from the metadata of these files.

Stored in the file ‘2021-09-14 NK cells_cycif test METADATA (exposure time, area, etc.)’ on google drive. 2021-09-14 NK cells_cycif test METADATA (exposure time, area, etc.)

NK cells and antibody validation (2021-11-15 NK cells_cycif)

New antibodies ordered to mark NK cells were tested:

NKp46 (NCR1) - SantaCruz, cat# sc-53599 (unconjugated)

CD56 - SantaCruz, cat# sc-7326 (unconjugated)

Also tested some antibodies that were already present in the lab but had not been validated yet, compiled in the ‘not currently recommended list’: CyCIF antibodies_Färkkilä_2021-09-15_filtered ]

CD45 to mark all immune cells, CD56, CD16, NCR1 to further identify the NK cells. (also to ensure targeting both the CD56dimCD16bright and the CD56brightCD16dim NK cell subsets)

IBA1, CD3D were used as positive controls since they worked well in the pilot test (2021-09-14).

CD4 included to check CD4+ NK cells based on https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16951326/

CD66b and CD138 were tested from the unvalidated antibodies as they fit the open slots as they matched the channels.

Information related to the project stored in Asana: “CyCIF NK cells  15112021” [https://app.asana.com/0/1201359271722976/board]

Antibody panel in Asana and also linked to google drive

Information Information present on Google drive ‘Projects’ folder -> ‘CyCIF’ -> ‘2021-11-15 NK cells_cycif’ [2021-11-15 NK cells_cycif] includes:

List of samples

Samples_NKcells_2021-11-15.xlsx

Used (Slides (3) and (4) sectioned from each paraffin-embedded tissue block – “Normal spleen1”; total 2 slides – handled by Sagarika.

Details and Protocol with specifics followed for that experiment

Details, Protocol_NKcells_2021-11-15

Channels (.csv file)

Channels_2021-11-15_normal_spleen1_slide3.csv

Channels_2021-11-15_normal_spleen1_slide4.csv

Antibody panel

Antibody panel_NKcells_2021-11-15

Tonsil tissue was previously used in some CyCIF experiments but now no FFPE blocks for tonsil were found in the lab, Ashwini confirmed they were borrowed from Anni. A recent update from Anni was received that there were no extra tonsil blocks or sectioned slides that could be borrowed (November, 2021) to be used for CyCIF. Since we have blocks for normal spleen tissue, we continued to use that for this CyCIF run as well. (Would be good to validate NK cell markers in tonsil tissue as well to target the different NK cells population subsets)

Antibody stainings were cross-checked using Protein Atlas to compare areas of the tissue where staining was to be expected in the tissue.

Finally, all the slides used in both CyCIF runs were photobleached and stored in 1X PBS (transparent container + vertical slide holder) - stored at +4C.

User guide

Created a user guide with detailed step-by-step instructions on how to use the Nikon scanning microscope to conduct t-CyCIF using the software implemented by Julia which consists of running jobs for the setup and for the CyCIF run, with instructions specific for the first day of scanning (critical step here to save the coordinate points of the region of interest) and for the subsequent cyclical days of scanning (the critical point here is to load the saved coordinate points of the region of interest saved on the day of the setup, for each and every individual slide). Verified by using it for the next CyCIF experiment, along with including changes based on Julia’s input.

Saved in google drive: ‘Projects’ folder -> ‘CyCIF’ -> ‘Instructions - NIKON SCANNER for CyCIF’ [https://docs.google.com/document/d/10QZaAVSPXGdNFtwq3j0t2v3bpRAkB6SvYo5-ks7Ia9Q/edit?usp=sharing]

Used Julia’s ‘NIS-CyCIF How to guide’ NIS-CyCIF How to guide for visual references of the scanner and the software before going to the scanner and to confirm steps to be followed and included in the detailed instructions: Instructions - NIKON SCANNER for CyCIF (updated on 2021-11-16)

Alternative antibodies needed for t-CyCIF

Investigated the antibodies used previously for CyCIF from all the stored antibody lists, CyCIF experiment plans and panels used and stored. Explored data on datacloud, google drive, and the (black) external hard drive to develop an updated list of the source of all the tested antibodies, observed results and validations, presence in the lab.

Compiled all the information from previous CyCIF experiments regarding the antibodies used and made separate lists of antibodies for CyCIF into sheets titled “not currently recommended antibodies” (inspired by cycif.org and their list) for those that were not surely working or not tested yet, and kept information in the existing sheet “Antibodies info” gathered about the working antibodies from Ashwini’s notes on these experiments, combining information for the antibodies in different experiments and from our observations after the pilot CyCIF run (e.g. dilutions used, specific antibodies and associated fluorochromes used, notes reported on the quality of staining).

[Experiment-wise info in ‘not currently recommended list’: CyCIF antibodies_Färkkilä_2021-09-15.xlsx]

[Filtered info in ‘not currently recommended list’: CyCIF antibodies_Färkkilä_2021-09-15_filtered ]

Planned experiments to validate some of the antibodies present in the lab in the next CYCIF run. Did this for antibodies - CD16, CD138 and CD66b.

Listed alternative options for antibodies that were reported not to be working in previous experiments, based on recommendations from cycif.org and availability of conjugated antibody options and reports in literature.

Searched for antibodies to be used as markers for Natural Killer (NK) cells – CD56 and NCR1.

CYTO for cell-type annotation

Used the tool CYTO, as taught by Matilda, for the purpose of cell-type annotations (generalized and specific annotations) of clusters of cells within the parent clusters: “T-cell” cluster, “Myeloid” cluster, and “Others” cluster, based on the dominant marker expression levels of each cluster that was deduced from the generated heat-maps. This was done for tumor-organoid pairs and drug-treated organoids (S085-S092).

Inventory and organization of samples and reagents in -80C and -20C freezers

Filled in and updated online inventory sheet “Cell culture reagents_2021-04-22" Cell culture reagents_2021-04-22.xlsx, Boxes: “-80 CYTOKINES”, “-80 GROWTH FACTORS”, “-80 Recombinant proteins”, “-80 Inhibitors and Drugs”.

Filled in gaps in other inventory sheets for stored samples such as:ONCOSYS-OVA Ascites -80C.xlsx, ONCOSYS-OVA LN snap-frozen tissues -80C.xlsx.

Updated map of boxes in -80C freezer, created template sheets for easy creation of new ones to be copied for the inventory excel sheets.

Tissue embedding into paraffin blocks and organizing blocks to be sent for sectioning and H&E staining

Learned to perform embedding of formalin-fixed tissue into paraffin blocks with Matilda. These FFPE blocks were then sent for sectioning and H&E staining.

Organized some remaining FFPE blocks that needed to be sent again either because it was not done yet or because they needed to be re-stained due to grid marks being visible.

Assistance in the form of various miscellaneous tasks:

These tasks included exploring previous data from past experiments and designing plans for future experiments, creating a project-outline figure in BioRender based on an existing figure, organizing data and record-keeping such as marking whether organoids were cultured for each sample, compiling images of organoid cultures taken on different days of each sample into one file for easier analysis and comparison, creating R plots for presentations, helping create a budget list searching information from past orders, searching information on antibody panels used in other papers, creating a list comparing antibodies and their details present in the lab (saved in the CyCIF folder in google drive: Antibodies used in other papers) making aliquots of reagents, preparing organoid media, etc.
